items.company: Netcracker Technology in Dubai, United Arab Emirates â Apply on Wuzzuf

warm_intro_candidates.name: Guruprasath E
warm_intro_candidates.title: Lead, Talent Acquisition at Netcracker Technology
warm_intro_candidates.why: Public LinkedIn result shows a Netcracker TA lead with 500+ connections and active Netcracker hiring posts. Best recruiter route for the Dubai Program Director / managed services mandate.

warm_intro_candidates.name: Sushmita Shastri
warm_intro_candidates.title: Senior Associate - Talent Acquisition at Netcracker Technology
warm_intro_candidates.why: Already present in local outreach history for Netcracker and public LinkedIn result confirms Netcracker TA/recruiting activity. Good secondary recruiter route if Guruprasath is not responsive.

queue_notes: No new queued companies were exported this Sunday; this was an older exported backlog item from 2026-04-08, now processed.
Local outreach history already had Netcracker people: Sushmita Shastri, Guruprasath E, Ashwini Gai, and Nitika Mathur. Public search validated Guruprasath and Sushmita as the strongest TA routes.

LinkedIn cookies imported into Camoufox, but LinkedIn still redirected to login, so profile validation used local history plus public LinkedIn snippets instead of an authenticated LinkedIn people search.
Role fit is moderate: senior transformation/program governance is strong, but telecom/BSS domain specificity makes this warmer as recruiter outreach than as direct executive sponsor outreach.
next_actions: Draft-first approach only; do not send connection requests without Ahmed approval.
Prioritize Guruprasath E, then Sushmita Shastri, with a concise note around Dubai Program Director, multi-country PMO, client governance, profitability, and digital transformation delivery.
If one accepts, follow with a short message asking whether the Dubai Program Director / managed services mandate is still active and who owns the hiring process.

Suggested connection note: Hi Guruprasath — I saw Netcracker’s Dubai Program Director mandate. I’ve led multi-country PMO and digital transformation portfolios across GCC banking/payments and healthcare, including steering governance and client delivery. Happy to connect if this is still active.

items.job_title: Head of Distribution Automation Global Delivery Center

warm_intro_candidates.name: Faizal Khan
warm_intro_candidates.title: Lead Talent Partner - COE at Hitachi Energy for UAE, Egypt and South Africa
warm_intro_candidates.why: Public LinkedIn result points to a Hitachi Energy talent partner covering UAE/Egypt/South Africa. Best direct recruiting route for a Dubai-based global delivery leadership role.

warm_intro_candidates.name: Helga-Carla Benegui (Botz)
warm_intro_candidates.title: Global Head of HR for Functions / Head of HR MEA at Hitachi Energy
warm_intro_candidates.why: Senior HR leader for MEA at Hitachi Energy. Good warm route for leadership-level fit, especially if the role sits across functions and geographies.

warm_intro_candidates.name: Nader Abdellatif
warm_intro_candidates.title: Hitachi Energy leader in Dubai with current full P&L responsibility
warm_intro_candidates.why: Business-side senior leader in Dubai with P&L responsibility. Good strategic sponsor route because the role itself has future P&L ownership and cross-functional delivery model design.

queue_notes: No new queued companies were exported this Sunday; this was an older exported backlog item from 2026-04-08, now processed.

The role is one of the strongest warm-intro candidates in the queue: blank-page global delivery model, P&L ownership, digital fulfillment/e-commerce, customer experience, and cross-functional operating model design all align with Ahmed’s transformation narrative.

Public sources show the role is/was posted across LinkedIn and job mirrors with Dubai included as a client-provided location. The Muse page says the job is no longer available, so outreach should position as relationship-building and verification, not assume the vacancy remains open.
LinkedIn cookies imported into Camoufox, but LinkedIn still redirected to login, so validation used local job data plus public LinkedIn snippets.
next_actions: Draft-first approach only; do not send connection requests without Ahmed approval.
Prioritize Faizal Khan first as the UAE/Egypt/South Africa talent partner; use Helga-Carla Benegui as the senior HR escalation route if Faizal does not respond.
Use Nader Abdellatif as the business-side connection target if Ahmed wants a strategic sponsor route rather than recruiter route.

Suggested connection note: Hi Faizal — I saw Hitachi Energy’s Distribution Automation global delivery leadership mandate in Dubai. I’ve led multi-country transformation/PMO portfolios across GCC healthcare and payments, with governance, delivery model design, and executive stakeholder ownership. Happy to connect.

items.job_title: Head of Transformation job in Dubai, United Arab Emirates - Cigna

warm_intro_candidates.name: Leah Cotterill
warm_intro_candidates.title: Chief Executive Officer, Middle East And Africa (outside KSA)
warm_intro_candidates.why: Regional CEO in Dubai with direct MEA ownership. Best senior sponsor path for a transformation mandate.

warm_intro_candidates.name: Sevda Esenturk
warm_intro_candidates.title: Head of HR, Cigna Healthcare International
warm_intro_candidates.why: Dubai-based HR leader responsible for International Health people strategy across MEA, APAC, Europe, and the US. Strongest HR route for role visibility.

warm_intro_candidates.name: Samer Taleb
warm_intro_candidates.title: Chief Financial Officer, Middle East And Africa / former Head of Strategy
warm_intro_candidates.why: Direct strategy and execution adjacency to the transformation office. Strong business-side sponsor candidate.

queue_notes: Local LinkedIn cookie file was present but browser session still required password, so profile validation relied on local workspace data plus active LinkedIn public profile fetches.
Cigna is the warmest queue item this week because Ahmed has live GCC healthcare transformation credibility and the role sits inside the same regional transformation context.
next_actions: Prioritize Leah Cotterill and Sevda Esenturk for connection requests.
Use a healthcare-transformation angle, not a generic job-seeker note.
If one connection is accepted, follow with a short note referencing MEA transformation, data modernization, and Ahmed's current SGH-group mandate.

warm_intro_candidates.name: Abs Khan
warm_intro_candidates.title: Director at Involved Solutions
warm_intro_candidates.why: Dubai-based director at the recruiting firm. Shared GCC tech hiring context and a Talabat connection make this the strongest warm-ish route.

warm_intro_candidates.name: Jack Gibson
warm_intro_candidates.title: Recruitment Consultant at Involved Solutions
warm_intro_candidates.why: Direct recruiter at the firm, useful if the PMO Director search is being run cross-border from London.

queue_notes: This looks cooler than Cigna because the company is a staffing/recruiting intermediary, not the end employer.
Abs Khan is still worth prioritizing because of his UAE location and prior Talabat recruiting experience, which creates a credible shared angle for Ahmed.
next_actions: Send the first connection request to Abs Khan with a shared Talabat and GCC transformation angle.
Treat this as recruiter outreach, not a direct business-leader introduction.
If no reply in 5 to 7 days, follow with Jack Gibson or ask Abs directly whether he owns the PMO Director mandate.
